top_categories.workout_context: 280 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 280 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
280 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Chase the Sun Norway's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,827ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
10,827ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Chase the Sun Norway's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 280 miles.
Chase the Sun Norway's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Tempo is Chase the Sun Norway's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 280 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Chase the Sun Norway. Train it until it feels boring.
At Chase the Sun Norway, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 280 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 280 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Chase the Sun Norway. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Endurance, and HVLI Extended to prepare you for 280 miles of mixed terrain in Bergen to Kristiansand, Norway.
